fixes_applied.original_issue: Regex::new(r"(?s)func\s+main\s*\(\s*\)\s*\{[^}]*\}").unwrap() at runtime in run_tier2_go() — would panic the benchmark runner if pattern were ever changed to invalid regex
fixes_applied.fix_applied: Hoisted regex to a `static RE_GO_MAIN: LazyLock<Regex>` with `.expect()` (compile-time intent documentation). Removed the per-call `.unwrap()`. Added `regexes_compile()` unit test that forces LazyLock initialisation and asserts the pattern matches `func main() { return }` and does not match `func helper()`.

fixes_applied.original_issue: Three eprintln! calls (lines 39, 42-46, 74) bypass tracing infrastructure — progress messages invisible to -v/-vv/-vvv structured log output
fixes_applied.fix_applied: Replaced all three eprintln! blocks with tracing::info! so review progress integrates with the verbosity system. The `println!` for formatted output on line 99 is intentional stdout and was left unchanged.

fixes_applied.original_issue: eprintln! on line 1911 (Results saved to ...) also bypasses tracing
fixes_applied.fix_applied: Converted the single save_results_json eprintln! to tracing::info! for consistency with review.rs fix. Benchmark progress eprintln! calls (run progress, task results) are intentional real-time terminal output and were left unchanged.

fixes_applied.original_issue: Security R5-W3: find -exec rm -rf and find -delete bypass check_blocked_patterns entirely because the leading token is `find`, not `rm`
fixes_applied.fix_applied: Added a `find` branch in check_blocked_patterns: (1) `-delete` flag is blocked as Critical with message 'find -delete can recursively delete files'; (2) `-exec rm` sub-invocation with recursive+force flags (parsed via existing collect_flags/has_recursive_force) is blocked as Critical with message 'find -exec rm -rf is equivalent to rm -rf'.

fixes_applied.original_issue: No regression tests for find -exec rm / find -delete bypass vectors
fixes_applied.fix_applied: Added 5 new tests: blocks_find_exec_rm_rf, blocks_find_exec_rm_rf_semicolon, blocks_find_delete, allows_find_read_only (negative case), blocks_find_exec_rm_nonrecursive. All pass.

fixes_applied.original_issue: WARNING: sync std::fs calls inside async context without spawn_blocking
fixes_applied.fix_applied: Deferred — architectural change requiring audit of all call sites. Out of scope for cleanup round.

fixes_applied.original_issue: WARNING: append_trace_event uses blocking fs I/O without spawn_blocking
fixes_applied.fix_applied: Deferred — architectural change. Out of scope for cleanup round.

fixes_applied.original_issue: WARNING: sh -c string construction instead of argv builders for auto-detected commands
fixes_applied.fix_applied: Deferred — architectural change requiring rewrite of detect_lint_command / detect_test_command. Out of scope.

fixes_applied.original_issue: WARNING: CodebaseIndex rebuilt on every tool invocation
fixes_applied.fix_applied: Deferred — requires adding shared Arc<RwLock<CodebaseIndex>> to AgentStack. Architectural change out of scope.

fixes_applied.original_issue: WARNING: fire-and-forget tokio::spawn for set_thinking_level/cycle_thinking/set_mode silently discards errors
fixes_applied.fix_applied: Deferred — requires event loop changes to surface errors as toast notifications. Out of scope for cleanup round.

summary: Fixed the critical Regex::new().unwrap() by hoisting to a LazyLock<Regex> static with a regexes_compile() test. Converted eprintln! progress messages in review.rs and benchmark.rs to tracing::info! so they integrate with the -v verbosity system. Plugged the security gap where `find -exec rm -rf` and `find -delete` bypassed the rm-rf blocklist in the command classifier, with 5 new regression tests. All 0 failures across the full workspace test suite.
